translations.descricao: Dementia is a syndrome characterized by progressive deterioration of cognitive functions, accompanied by psychiatric symptoms and behavioral disorders that lead to progressive and irreversible incapacity. It is the second leading cause of neurodegenerative dementia, with significant impact on the quality of life of patients and families. There is evidence of an association between periodontitis and dementia, recommending prevention and integrated management. Early diagnosis uses biomarkers such as PET imaging and blood tests for associated conditions, such as Alzheimer's disease. Ethical considerations include nutritional management and prevention of adverse drug reactions in the elderly. Genetic investigation is recommended in selected cases. Consensuses emphasize controversial enteral nutrition in advanced stages.
translations.sus.indicacao: Cognitive assessment for elderly individuals with suspicion of cognitive decline or risk factors, without a routine population screening program, prioritizing early diagnosis in primary care
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Elderly ≥65 years with suggestive symptoms, comorbidities, or risk factors such as polypharmacy and comorbidities
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Diagnosis using PET imaging and blood biomarkers for neurodegenerative conditions associated with dementia, including Alzheimer's and dementia with Lewy bodies; prevention of adverse drug events and nutritional management
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Globally, the prevalence of dementia in adults >60 years is approximately 5-8%, affecting millions due to population aging
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Annual incidence of about 1-2% in elderly >65 years, with increase in populations with comorbidities such as periodontitis
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Dementia contributes to about 7% of deaths in the elderly, with significant impact on quality of life and autonomy
